name: Harold Land
abstract: Harold de Vance Land (December 18, 1928 – July 27, 2001) was an American hard bop and post-bop tenor saxophonist. Land developed his hard bop playing with the Max Roach/Clifford Brown band into a personal, modern style, often rivalling Clifford Brown's instrumental ability with his own inventive and whimsical solos.

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

works.t: Anything Goes: I Get a Kick Out of You

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: So my first group... was Billy Higgins, was the drummer, Billy Childs was the pianist, George Bohannon was a trombone, Oscar Brashear was a trumpet, Harold Land was the saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: So me and Harold, we started working at this club... We did a lot of albums, a lot of stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: Red Mitchell and Harold Land and I started playing with everybody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: go to see ... Harold Land, Bobby Hutcherson, Cedar, Buster, Billy Higgins. So you know they’d remember. “Oh those are Jackie’s little guys.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, they were from California, Harold Land, Teddy Edwards, Charles Lloyd. Those are the people that you would recognize.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: So that left Max and George Morrow, the bass player. So Donald Byrd, Sonny Rollins and myself, we joined Max’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
